title: Autumn Durald Arkapaw Wins Best Cinematography Oscar for Sinners - Deadline
text: Cinematographer Autumn Durald Arkapaw made history at the 2026 Academy Awards, becoming the first woman to win the Oscar for Best Cinematography. She won for her work on director Ryan Coogler's film Sinners, defeating competitors including Dan Laustsen (Frankenstein), Darius Khondji (Marty Supreme), Michael Bauman (One Battle After Another), and Adolpho Veloso (Train Dreams). This achievement is particularly significant as Arkapaw was only the third woman ever nominated in this category, following Rachel Morrison, Ari Wegner, and Mandy Walker. Host Conan O'Brien highlighted the moment as incredible. In her acceptance speech, Arkapaw emphasized the importance of female support: I really want all the women in the room to stand up because I don't get here without you guys...moments like this happen because of you guys. She credited director Coogler for his trust and described the project as deeply personal. Arkapaw previously collaborated with Coogler on Wakanda Forever and called the Sinners experience particularly meaningful due to Coogler's personal investment in the story. The win was somewhat surprising, as Arkapaw had lost the cinematography award at the BAFTAs and both the British and American cinematography societies to Bauman.
